textContent:
A1. The allomorphs of in/at is "da" or "ta".
A2. "da" if root ends in a vowel, "ta" if root ends in a consonant (voiceless?)

B1. Hypercorrection. People applied the conjugation of sing/sang/sung to another verb that also ends in -ing.

Syntactic change
If this keeps up, the word order might be free and not restricted to VSO anymore, however the order might shift to focus (parang Basque)
title: Activity 5
textContentHtml:
A1. The allomorphs of in/at is "da" or "ta".

A2. "da" if root ends in a vowel, "ta" if root ends in a consonant (voiceless?)


B1. Hypercorrection. People applied the conjugation of sing/sang/sung to another verb that also ends in -ing.


Syntactic change

If this keeps up, the word order might be free and not restricted to VSO anymore, however the order might shift to focus (parang Basque)
